page: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance includes 2 physical cores with single-thread execution per core. It is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a 320 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mixed profile. The AMD EPYC Genoa processor delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, placing in the top 10%. However, multi-core CPU performance drops to the middle 50% average, and overall PassMark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25% weak category. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform strongly, ranking in the top 25%. Conversely, memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is weak, though large block read and write bandwidths maintain average speeds.

The server exhibits performance bottlenecks in database and web serving workloads. PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations both rank in the bottom 25%, while static web serving throughput is poor, falling into the bottom 10%. Large-model LLM inference is also highly constrained, with 7B model text generation scoring in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high memory allocation of 16.0 GB and substantial NVMe storage for a 2-vCPU footprint, though the shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention. This hardware profile is best suited for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads, such as caching layers, lightweight background workers, or basic file processing tasks that benefit from single-threaded performance and generous memory capacity rather than sustained multi-core execution.
description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 2 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC Genoa processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng benchmarks, though its multi-core and database performance metrics fall into the lower quartiles. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core provides excellent resource density for memory-bound tasks, but the shared CPU allocation and weak database throughput limit its suitability for heavy transactional databases or high-traffic web serving. It is best suited for memory-intensive, compute-light applications, single-threaded file processing, and caching workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd is an AMD EPYC Genoa server with 2 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 320 GB NVMe storage, offering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive, compute-light workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd features 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 320 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-optimized cloud workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining strong single-core AMD performance with generous NVMe storage for memory-bound, compute-light workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Genoa processor with 2 shared vCPUs and 2.0 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for fast local data access
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Suited for memory-intensive caching, file processing, and light background tasks
